content.problemStatement:
There are NNN workers; each worker is of one of the following three types:


A translator translates some text from Chef's language to another language.
An author writes some text in Chef's language.
An author-translator can both write a text in Chef's language and translate it to another language.
Chef wants to have some text written and translated into some language (different from Chef's language). Chef can't do either of those tasks, but he can hire workers. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), if he hires the iii-th worker, he must pay that worker cic_ici​ coins.

Help Chef find the minimum total number of coins he needs to pay to have a text written and translated. It is guaranteed that it is possible to write and translate a text.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN denoting the number of workers.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers c1,c2,...,cNc_1, c_2, ..., c_Nc1​,c2​,...,cN​ denoting the numbers of coins Chef has to pay each hired worker.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers t1,t2,...,tNt_1, t_2, ..., t_Nt1​,t2​,...,tN​ denoting the types of workers. For each valid iii, the iii-th worker is a translator if ti=1t_i = 1ti​=1, an author if ti=2t_i = 2ti​=2 or an author-translator if ti=3t_i = 3ti​=3.


Output Format

Print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of coins Chef has to pay.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer N denoting the number of workers. The second line contains N space-separated integers c1, c2, ..., cN denoting the numbers of coins Chef has to pay each hired worker. The third line contains N space-separated integers t1, t2, ..., tN denoting the types of workers. For each valid i, the i-th worker is a translator if ti = 1, an author if ti = 2 or an author-translator if ti = 3.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of coins Chef has to pay to have a text written and translated.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Chef can hire 2 workers: worker 1, who is a translator, and worker 2, who is an author. In total, he pays them 1+3=4 coins.
Chef can hire 1 worker: worker 3, who is an author-translator. In total, he pays them 2 coins.

solution.approach: To find the minimum total number of coins Chef has to pay, we need to consider the costs of hiring different types of workers. We can iterate through the workers and keep track of the minimum cost for each type. Then, we can calculate the minimum total cost by considering the minimum cost of hiring a translator and an author, and the minimum cost of hiring an author-translator.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) — where N is the number of workers. We iterate through the workers once to find the minimum costs for each type.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the minimum costs for each type.

code.Python3:
n = int(input())
c = list(map(int, input().split()))
t = list(map(int, input().split()))

at=float('inf')
tr=float('inf')
attr=float('inf')

for i in range(n):
    if t[i]==1:
        tr=min(tr,c[i])
    elif t[i]==2:
        at=min(at,c[i])
    else:
        attr=min(attr,c[i])

print(min(tr+at, attr))
